english: spend, lay out, expend; skyag-sgó (skyag sgo) expenditure, skyag-tó (skyag to) account of expenses. — 3. W.: *skyag táṅ-ċe* (*skyag tang ce*) to slaughter, to murder.
pavement, clay-floor, mud-floor Lex., Cs.; skaṅ-núl byéd-pa (skang nul byed pa) to pave, to plaster (Sch. also; to rub, polish).

(cf. skyób-pa (skyob pa)) Ssk. शरण protection, defence, help, assistance; me-čú-la skyabs (me chu la skyabs) is a protection against water and fire; skyabs méd-do (skyabs med do) I am (or: he is etc.) lost! skyabs byéd-pa (skyabs byed pa), skyábs-su ⸰gyúr-ba (skyabs su 'gyur ba) ccgp. to protect, help, save a person, frq. with srog-gi (srog gi) added; skyábs-su ⸰gró-ba (skyabs su 'gro ba) eleg. mčiba (mchi ba), W.: *skyab čól-la yóṅ-ċe* (*skyab chol la yong ce*) to seek help, míi (mi'i) or mi-la (mi la) of some body, skyabs-⸰grós (skyabs 'gros) 1. the seeking of help, शरण गमन 2. the formula Saṅs-rgyás-kyi skyabs-su mčío (sangs rgyas kyi skyabs su mchi'o), čós-kyi sky. mčío (chos kyi sky. mchi'o), dge-⸰dún-gyi sky. mčío (dge 'dun gyi sky. mchi'o), the Buddhistic creed or confession of faith. Comp. skyabs-mgón (skyabs mgon) helper, protector, deliverer; this is applied to certain highly esteemed and respected persons, mythological as well as living, m f.; Chr. Pr. use it for Saviour, Redeemer, Christ. — skyabs-⸰grós (skyabs 'gros) v. above. — skyabs-gnás (skyabs gnas) 1. place of refuge, shelter; also of persons, = helper, frq ; mí-la skyabs-gnás byéd-pa (mi la skyabs gnas byed pa) Mil. to take refuge to a person, to seek his assistance. 2. seld. for skyábs-su gnás-pa (skyabs su gnas pa) client, ṅá-yi skyábs-gnas pó-mo-rnams (nga yi skyabs gnas po mo rnams) all my clients, men and women Glr. — skyabs-sbyín (skyabs sbyin) a gesture of the right hand, like that for giving benediction Glr. — skyabs-yúl (skyabs yul) = skyabs-gnás (skyabs gnas). 1.

Sch.: snipe, wood-cock; skyar-čúṅ (skyar chung) Sch.: 'a large snipe' (??); skyár-mo (skyar mo) Sch. heron; skyar-léb (skyar leb) Sch. spoon-bill; ču-skyar (chu skyar) Cs. duck, Sch.: bittern, but the कादम्ब of the Lex., 'a kind of goose' speaks in favour of Cs.
a changing of abode; skyas ⸰débs-pa (skyas 'debs pa) to change one's dwelling-place (cf. skya-ba (skya ba)), skyas čén-po ⸰débs-pa (skyas chen po 'debs pa) to die

Cs.: the outward side of a skin or hide (opp. to śa (sha)); skyi gyá-ba (skyi gya ba) to shiver, tremble with fear Cs. Comp.: skyi-dkár (skyi dkar) Cs. dressed leather; hide. — skyi-lpágs (skyi lpags) Sch.: chamois, wash-leather. — skyi-búṅ (skyi bung) Mil.? — skyi-bún (skyi bun) prob. an itching of the skin Mil.? — skyi-śá (skyi sha) 1. outward and inward side of a hide. 2. Sch.: the anus.

I. sbst. 1. a medicinal plant Med. - 2. also *kyi-u (kyi u), pi-líṅ kyi-u (pi ling kyi u),* potato C. II. vb. pf. bskyis (bskyis), ft. bskyi (bskyi), imp. skyis (skyis) to borrow, esp. money or goods (cf. gyár-ba (gyar ba) and skyín-pa (skyin pa)).
to hickup; skuiṅ-bu (skuing bu) the hickup Med.

vb., sbst., adj.: to be happy, happiness (Ssk. सौख्य), happy; skyíd-do (skyid do) (I, thou etc.) am, art etc. happy; bdé-źiṅ skyid-la (bde zhing skyid la) being happy and glad; skyid-pai nyí-ma (skyid pa'i nyi ma) sun of felicity, propitious day Glr.; skyid-po (skyid po) = skyid-pa (skyid pa) adj., frq., skyid-de-ba (skyid de ba) id. Tar. 5, 19. Comp. skyíd-glu (skyid glu) song of joy. — skyid-mgo (skyid mgo) beginning of happiness Mil. — skyid-sdúg (skyid sdug) good and ill luck, happiness and misery; skyid sdug ji byuṅ kyaṅ (skyid sdug ji byung kyang) whatever may happen Glr.; skyid sdug bsré-ba (skyid sdug bsre ba) to share pleasure and pain. — skyid-čú (skyid chu) n. of the tributary of the Ya-ru-tsaṅ-po (ya ru tsang po), on which Lhasa is situated.

skyin-pa (skyin pa), W. *skyiṅ-po* (*skying po*), resp. kar-skyin (kar skyin) a loan, a thing borrowed; money advanced without interest; skyin-pa skyí-ba (skyin pa skyi ba) to ask a loan; ṅá-la ⸰di skyin-du ⸰tsal (nga la 'di skyin du 'tsal) he asked me to lend him this Dzl.; skyin-pa lén-pa (skyin pa len pa) Cs. to take on credit; skyin-pa spród-pa (skyin pa sprod pa), ⸰jál-ba ('jal ba) to pay back or return a loan Cs.; nór-skyin (nor skyin) a loan of
